2605.13037.title: MAP: A Map-then-Act Paradigm for Long-Horizon Interactive Agent Reasoning
2605.13037.paper_summary: Current interactive LLM agents rely on goal-conditioned stepwise planning, where environmental understanding is acquired reactively during execution rather than established beforehand. This temporal inversion leads to Delayed Environmental Perception: agents must infer environmental constraints through trial-and-error, resulting in an Epistemic Bottleneck that traps them in inefficient failure cycles. Inspired by human affordance perception and cognitive map theory, we propose the Map-then-Act Paradigm (MAP), a plug-and-play framework that shifts environment understanding before execution. MAP consists of three stages: (1) Global Exploration, acquiring environment-general priors; (2) Task-Specific Mapping, constructing a structured cognitive map; and (3) Knowledge-Augmented Execution, solving tasks grounded on the map. Experiments show consistent gains across benchmarks and LLMs. On ARC-AGI-3, MAP enables frontier models to surpass near-zero baseline performance in 22 of 25 game environments. We further introduce MAP-2K, a dataset of map-then-act trajectories, and show that training on it outperforms expert execution traces, suggesting that understanding environments is more fundamental than imitation.
2605.13037.paper_summary_zh: 目前的互動 LLM 代理依賴於目標條件的逐步規劃，其中環境理解是在執行過程中反應性地獲得，而不是事先建立。這種時間上的逆轉導致了延遲的環境感知：代理必須通過反覆試驗推斷環境約束，這導致了認知瓶頸，使它們陷入低效的失敗循環。受到人類可供性感知和認知地圖理論的啟發，我們提出了地圖-然後-行動範式（MAP），這是一個即插即用的框架，將環境理解的過程提前到執行之前。MAP 包含三個階段：（1）全球探索，獲取環境通用的先驗知識；（2）任務特定映射，構建結構化的認知地圖；以及（3）知識增強執行，基於地圖解決任務。實驗顯示在基準測試和 LLM 上都有一致的增益。在 ARC-AGI-3 上，MAP 使前沿模型在 25 個遊戲環境中的 22 個超越接近零的基線性能。我們進一步介紹了 MAP-2K，一個地圖-然後-行動軌跡的數據集，並顯示在其上訓練的表現優於專家執行痕跡，這表明理解環境比模仿更為根本。
2605.13037.author: Yuxin Liu et.al.
2605.13037.authors: Yuxin Liu, Ziang Ye, Yueqing Sun, Mingye Zhu, Jinwei Xiao, Zhuowen Han, Qi GU, Xunliang Cai, Lei Zhang
